notes:
bỏ = to leave, quit, or give up something. It’s used when you stop doing something or decide not to continue.

It’s very flexible: you can bỏ + noun (give up/throw away something) or bỏ + verb (stop doing something: bỏ học, bỏ ăn). It can also mean “discard” or “not include” depending on context.

It often carries a feeling of letting go or cutting something off.

Close ideas: ngừng (stop – more neutral), từ bỏ (give up – stronger, more emotional).
exampleSentences: Nếu em không thích công việc này nữa, em có thể bỏ.
Anh đã quyết định bỏ thành phố để sống gần gia đình hơn.
Chị ấy không nên bỏ cơ hội tốt như thế này.

translation.en: Do you want to leave this job?

content.sentence: Bạn có ___ công việc này không?

notes_md:
**bỏ** = to leave, quit, or give up something. It’s used when you stop doing something or decide not to continue.

It’s very flexible: you can **bỏ + noun** (give up/throw away something) or **bỏ + verb** (stop doing something: bỏ học, bỏ ăn). It can also mean “discard” or “not include” depending on context.

It often carries a feeling of *letting go* or *cutting something off*.


Close ideas: **ngừng** (stop – more neutral), **từ bỏ** (give up – stronger, more emotional).

translations.en: to leave out, to abandon, to give up
translations.vi: bỏ ra, từ bỏ, không giữ lại

example.en: I forgot to take the keys at home.
example.es: Olvidé llevar las llaves a casa.
example.vi: Tôi đã bỏ quên chìa khóa ở nhà.

definitions.en: to leave out, to abandon, to give up
definitions.vi: bỏ ra, từ bỏ, không giữ lại

exampleSentencesTranslated: If you don't like this job anymore, you can leave.
He decided to leave the city to live closer to his family.
She shouldn't leave such a good opportunity.
